controls.title: ICT risk management framework
controls.summary: Financial entities must have a sound, comprehensive and well-documented ICT risk management framework as part of their overall risk management system.

controls.title: ICT risk management — systems, protocols and tools
controls.summary: Entities must use and maintain updated ICT systems, protocols and tools that: minimise impact of ICT risk; ensure resilience, continuity, and availability; and allow detection, prevention, and response to events.

controls.title: Protection and prevention
controls.summary: Entities must implement security controls including encryption, MFA, access management, network segmentation, vulnerability scanning, and patch management as part of their protection strategy.

controls.title: Detection
controls.summary: Mechanisms to promptly detect anomalous activities, including network traffic analysis, log monitoring, and SIEM integration, must be in place to identify potential ICT-related incidents.

controls.title: Response and recovery
controls.summary: Entities must have ICT business continuity plans including BCP, DRP, communication plans, and recovery time objectives (RTO). Plans must be tested periodically.

controls.title: ICT-related incident management process
controls.summary: Entities must establish a comprehensive ICT-related incident management process to detect, manage, record, classify, report, and resolve all ICT-related incidents.

controls.title: Classification of ICT-related incidents and cyber threats
controls.summary: Entities must classify ICT-related incidents and cyber threats by materiality criteria: service disruption, number of clients affected, data loss duration, geographic spread, and economic impact.

controls.title: Reporting of major ICT-related incidents to competent authorities
controls.summary: Major ICT-related incidents must be reported: initial notification within 4 hours, intermediate report within 72 hours, and final report within one month.

controls.title: Harmonised testing of ICT tools and systems
controls.summary: Financial entities must establish a sound testing programme covering assessment, scanning, gap analysis, and vulnerability testing. Testing plans must be risk-based and approved by management.

controls.title: General requirements for TLPT
controls.summary: Entities identified by their competent authority must conduct threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) at least every 3 years using qualified testers, covering live production systems.

controls.title: General principles of ICT third-party risk management
controls.summary: Entities must manage ICT third-party risk as an integral component of ICT risk. A register of all ICT third-party contracts must be maintained with full due diligence assessments.

controls.title: Key contractual provisions for ICT third-party services
controls.summary: Contracts with ICT third-party providers must include security requirements, incident notification obligations, subcontracting restrictions, audit rights, exit strategy, and SLAs.
